items.briefing:
Australian authorities have issued a warning about a global hacking campaign actively targeting websites running vulnerable content management systems (CMS) like WordPress. Organisations running websites should ensure their CMS software, themes, and plugins are fully up to date. Separately, a new Android malware called RedHook can silently gain deep access to infected devices using a wireless debugging feature — a reminder to keep Android devices updated and avoid installing apps from unofficial sources.

IT teams using Progress ShareFile should urgently review guidance to shut down exposed servers due to a credible, active threat being reported.

**Practical tip:** Check that your website's CMS and all plugins are updated to the latest version — outdated software is one of the most common ways hackers gain access.

featured.title: Australia warns of global campaign targeting vulnerable CMS platforms - BleepingComputer
featured.quote: Australian authorities warn of cyber attacks targeting vulnerable content management systems globally.
